137.reason: [Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the code still sets tokenType to AND, which would incorrectly add an AND token to the token stream even though an error has been recorded. The code should skip adding the token when an error occurs.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the code still sets tokenType to AND, which would incorrectly add an AND token to the token stream even though an error has been recorded. The code should skip adding the token when an error occurs.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the token type should not be set to AND. The code should skip setting tokenType or handle the error differently to avoid adding an incorrect token.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This line executes even when a single '&' is detected (error case), adding an incorrect token. Should be conditional or valueChar should be set false.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Incorrectly assigns a valid token type and adds it to the stream even when a lexical error is detected.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token, which should not happen. The lexer should skip adding a token for erroneous inputs.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.AND and treats it as a valid token, which should not happen. The lexer should skip adding this invalid token to the token stream.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is recorded but the lexer still sets tokenType to OR and proceeds to add a token, which is incorrect behavior for an erroneous lexical element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the code still assigns TokenType.OR and will output it as a valid token, which is incorrect. Invalid tokens should not be added to the token stream.]

10.reason: [Using available() is unreliable for determining file size; may lead to incomplete read or zero-length byte array. Also if file does not exist, FileNotFoundException is thrown, but the catch block only rethrows RuntimeException, causing no output to be written.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using available() is unreliable for determining file size; may lead to incomplete read or zero-length byte array. Also if file does not exist, FileNotFoundException is thrown, but the catch block only rethrows RuntimeException, causing no output to be written.]
